objective: Probe VPI C++ Remap/WarpMap as the next Orin NX heterogeneous-video-compute extension after Python Remap native aborts, and decide whether it can support future local/mesh warp work.

entry_context.project_positioning: Jetson Orin NX heterogeneous video compute and device-side dataflow optimization, with EIS as the representative workload.
entry_context.why_this_route: Python VPI Remap aborted in the native binding, but that does not prove C++ Remap is impossible. VPI C++ Remap/WarpMap is a plausible operator-level path toward future mesh/local warp and parallax-boundary work.
entry_context.quality_anchor: resid_r15_s07 remains frozen and is not modified by this loop.
entry_context.current_device_boundary: Accepted C++ MMAPI/VPI/NVENC path remains block-linear main chain plus pitch-linear NV12_ER scratch; this loop starts with standalone C++ Remap, not immediate MMAPI integration.

result.decision: VPI C++ Remap is a positive module/operator result on this Jetson. CPU and CUDA backends work, CUDA beats OpenCV CPU on tested identity and local-wave maps, and NV12_ER CPU/CUDA works, making Remap plausible for future pitch-linear scratch integration.

format_boundary.nv12_er_vic: pre-convert from BGR8 failed in this probe; native NV12/NV12_ER VIC Remap is not validated

result.claim_boundary: Module/operator-level C++ Remap evidence only. No full-pipeline EIS, zero-copy, mesh-quality, or MMAPI integration claim.
completed_tasks: P4 OpenCV vs VPI C++ Remap benchmark

frozen_variables: no claim of full real-time EIS
official NVIDIA VPI samples and public docs are preferred references
